title: Claude Opus 4.7 Outperforms ChatGPT-5.5 in Reasoning Depth Across Seven AI Tests
summary: Claude Opus 4.7 Outperforms ChatGPT-5.5 in Reasoning Depth Across Seven AI Tests Claude Opus 4.7 Outperforms ChatGPT-5.5 in Reasoning Depth Across Seven AI Tests April 25, 2026 April 25, 2026 35 OpenAI released GPT-5.5 on April 23, just days after Anthropic unveiled Claude Opus 4.7, setting u
content: Claude Opus 4.7 Outperforms ChatGPT-5.5 in Reasoning Depth Across Seven AI Tests [...] Claude Opus 4.7 Outperforms ChatGPT-5.5 in Reasoning Depth Across Seven AI Tests [...] OpenAI released GPT-5.5 on April 23, just days after Anthropic unveiled Claude Opus 4.7, setting up a direct clash between two rival visions of what the next-generation AI assistant should do. [...] , and clean [...] To settle the question, a hands-on comparison ran both models through seven deliberately difficult prompts spanning probability, physics estimation, coding, and real-world problem-solving. The tests were designed not just to check for correct answers, but to expose how each model thinks through ambiguity, assumptions, and multi-step logic. [...] Beyond these hand-crafted challenges, benchmark data from Mashable painted a more nuanced picture. On the Arena leaderboard — which reflects real-user preferences in side-by-side comparisons — Claude Opus 4.7 Thinking held the top overall spot, suggesting users favor its tone, clarity, and thoughtfulness in open-ended interactions. [...] Yet on verified technical benchmarks, GPT-5.5 consistently edged ahead. It scored higher on Terminal-Bench 2.0 (82.vs 69.), BrowseComp (84.vs 79.), and both ARC-AGI-1 (94.vs ) and ARC-AGI-2 (83.vs 68.). The only exception was GPQA Diamond, where Claude Opus 4.7 led slightly at 94.to GPT-5.5’s 93.. [...] On Humanity’s Last Exam — a notoriously tough test of expert-level knowledge — the split depended on tool use. Without tools, GPT-5.5 scored 40.to Claude’s 31.. With tools enabled, Claude narrowed the gap and pulled ahead at 54.to GPT-5.5’s 52., suggesting it may integrate external information more effectively when allowed to browse or compute. [...] These results highlight a fundamental divergence: GPT-5.5 appears optimized for benchmark performance and structured task completion, while Claude Opus 4.7 excels in user-facing scenarios where reasoning transparency and consistency matter. [...] Based on the tests, Claude Opus 4.7 has an edge in advanced and agentic coding scenarios, while GPT-5.5 performs better on general coding benchmarks like Terminal-Bench 2.0. [...] Yes — on Humanity’s Last Exam, GPT-5.5 leads without tools, but Claude Opus 4.7 pulls ahead when both models are allowed to browse or compute, suggesting stronger tool integration in certain contexts.

title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains
summary: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains IT Brief India - Technology news for CIOs & IT decision-makers OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains Fri, 24th Apr 2026 (Yesterday) By Mark Tarre, News Chief OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 for ChatGPT and Codex users, with API
content: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains [...] OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains [...] Fri, 24th Apr 2026 (Yesterday) [...] OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 for ChatGPT and Codex users, with API access to follow. [...] OpenAI says the model is built to handle broader, multi-step tasks with less direct prompting. According to the company, GPT-5.5 performs strongly in coding, online research, data analysis, document creation, spreadsheet work and software operation, while maintaining the same per-token latency as GPT-5.4 in real-world serving. [...] A large part of the update centres on software engineering. OpenAI says GPT-5.5 scored 82.on Terminal-Bench 2.0, 58.on SWE-Bench Pro, and outperformed GPT-5.4 on its internal Expert-SWE evaluation for long-horizon coding tasks. [...] These tests are designed to measure how models handle command-line workflows, resolve GitHub issues and manage coding work that can take humans many hours. OpenAI says GPT-5.5 improved on GPT-5.4 across all three evaluations while using fewer tokens. [...] OpenAI also presents GPT-5.5 as a tool for broader office work. It says the same improvements that help with coding also support research, information gathering, output checking and turning raw material into documents, spreadsheets and slide presentations. [...] For benchmarked knowledge work, OpenAI says GPT-5.5 scored 84.on GDPval, 78.on OSWorld-Verified and 98.on Tau2-bench Telecom without prompt tuning. It also reported scores of 60.on FinanceAgent, 88.on internal investment banking modelling tasks and 54.on OfficeQA Pro. [...] OpenAI says GPT-5.5 also posted gains in scientific and technical research workflows, including genetics, quantitative biology and bioinformatics. It cited improvements over GPT-5.4 on GeneBench and said GPT-5.5 achieved leading performance among models with published scores on BixBench. [...] The company also said an internal version of GPT-5.5, used with a custom harness, helped discover a new proof related to off-diagonal Ramsey numbers, a topic in combinatorics. OpenAI says the result was later verified in Lean.

title: Google DeepMind launches Deep Research Max autonomous AI research agent
summary: Google DeepMind launches Deep Research Max autonomous AI research agent | ETIH EdTech News — EdTech Innovation Hub Google's new AI agent will run 160 searches while you sleep and hand you the report by morning 22 Apr New Gemini 3.1 Pro-powered agents hit public preview with MCP support, na
content: Google DeepMind launches Deep Research Max autonomous AI research agent | ETIH EdTech News — EdTech Innovation Hub [...] New Gemini 3.1 Pro-powered agents hit public preview with MCP support, native charts and multimodal inputs, as Google positions Deep Research as an enterprise workflow engine for finance and life sciences. [...] Google DeepMind has launched Gemini 3.1 Pro-powered Deep Research and Deep Research Max agents in public preview via the Gemini API. [...] Google DeepMind has launched two new autonomous research agents, Deep Research and Deep Research Max, in public preview via the Gemini API. Built on Gemini 3.1 Pro, the agents search the open web, user file uploads and connected data sources through Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, generate charts and infographics natively, and consult over 100 sources in a single task. [...] Google is positioning the release as a move beyond summarization into an enterprise workflow layer for finance, life sciences and market research, with named financial data partners already building MCP integrations. [...] The standard Deep Research agent replaces the December preview release and is optimized for speed and lower cost, aimed at interactive user-facing products. Deep Research Max is the heavier option, designed for asynchronous background workflows such as overnight due diligence reports, and uses extended test-time compute to iteratively search and refine its output. [...] Max runs around 160 search queries per task, according to Philipp Schmid, AI Developer Experience at Google DeepMind, who announced the update on LinkedIn. The two new models are "deep-research-preview-04-2026" and "deep-research-max-preview-04-2026". Schmid reported benchmark scores of 93.3 percent on DeepSearchQA for web research and 85.9 percent on BrowseComp for hard-fact retrieval. [...] Chief Strategist [...] set out the framing [...] "Two speeds with two purposes. The regular Deep Research is the quick approach: It [...] s fast, cheap and still gets you a solid answer. Max is the patient AI who works through the night [...] you wake up to something exhaustive," he posted [...] Hoyne said [...] agents pull in user files as well as Google data, generate charts and infographics in-line, and show users the plan before execution so they can "tweak, you redirect, you say 'focus here!', etc." [...] Google's new AI agent will run 160 searches while you sleep and hand you the report by morning

title: Anthropic resolves performance degradation in Claude Code models
summary: Anthropic resolves performance degradation in Claude Code models Anthropic resolves performance degradation in Claude Code models April 25, 2026 April 25, 2026 13 Developers across the tech industry began noticing a troubling pattern in late March: the code generated by Anthropic’s Claude mod
content: Anthropic resolves performance degradation in Claude Code models [...] Anthropic resolves performance degradation in Claude Code models [...] tech industry began [...] What users initially dismissed as fatigue or imagination turned out to be a real degradation in performance, traced by Anthropic to three separate changes made to its Claude Code agent and related tools over a six-week span. The company confirmed the issues affected its Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6, and Opus 4.7 models, but not its underlying API, and said all were resolved by April 20. [...] The first issue emerged on March 4, when Anthropic lowered the default reasoning effort from “high” to “medium” to reduce latency that had made the interface appear frozen for some users. While intended to improve responsiveness, the change backfired by limiting the model’s capacity for complex reasoning. After user feedback indicated a preference for higher default intelligence, Anthropic reverted the setting on April 7, allowing users to manually reduce effort when speed was prioritized over depth. [...] The final adjustment came on April 16, when a system prompt was added to curb verbosity by limiting text between tool calls to 25 words and final responses to 100 words unless detail was required. Though meant to streamline output, the constraint clashed with other prompting techniques and degraded code generation quality, particularly in structured tasks. Anthropic acknowledged the tradeoff and reverted the prompt on April 20. [...] Context: The degradation coincided with the release of Opus 4.6 in February, which had initially been praised for its reasoning capabilities but later showed signs of regression under real-world usage. [...] In response to the broader user experience issues, Anthropic announced on April 23 that it would reset usage limits for all subscribers, a move intended to restore confidence in the platform after the period of instability. [...] Anthropic states that all three identified issues were resolved by April 20, and that the model has returned to its intended performance levels; however, the company and independent auditors continue to recommend that users manually review and test any AI-generated code for correctness and security before deployment.

title: From GPT-5.5 to DeepSeek V4: How Developers Are Building Smarter AI Agents with Multi-Model Routing in 2026
summary: From GPT-5.5 to DeepSeek V4: How Developers Are Building Smarter AI Agents with Multi-Model Routing in 2026 - IssueWire From GPT-5.5 to DeepSeek V4: How Developers Are Building Smarter AI Agents with Multi-Model Routing in 2026 Singapore, Singapore Apr 25, 2026 (Issuewire.com) - April 2026 was t
content: 25, 20 [...] (Issuewire.com) - [...] . GPT- [...] on April [...] 3. DeepSeek V4 [...] dropped 24 [...] .7 launched on April 16. [...] 3.1 Pro, L [...] wen 3, Gemma 4 — all within the same six- [...] window. For [...] agents, the message is clear: the era of [...] committing is over. The era of multi-model routing has arrived. [...] DeepSeek V4 Preview (DeepSeek, April 24, 2026) is the release that may define Q2 2026. The model ships in two variants: V4-Pro (1.6 trillion parameters, 49 billion active, MIT license) and V4-Flash (284 billion parameters, 13 billion active). Priced at $0.14 per million input tokens for Flash and $1.74 for Pro, it is the cheapest frontier-class model ever released publicly. Independent benchmarks place V4-Pro within 7–8 points of Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 on SWE-bench — a gap that has narrowed from 15+ points just a year ago. For cost-sensitive production workloads, V4 changes the economics of AI deployment fundamentally. [...] Llama 4 Scout (Meta, Q1 2026) ships with a 10 million token context window — a number that makes even enterprise document processing constraints effectively obsolete. Fully open-weight and free to self-host, Llama 4 Maverick outperforms previous-generation closed-source models on major benchmarks while running on a single H100 GPU. For teams that need data sovereignty, self-hosting, or processing entire codebases and legal document collections in a single pass, Llama 4 Scout is unmatched. [...] The frontier is not standing still. Claude Mythos — Anthropic's next model, currently restricted to 50 partner organizations under Project Glasswing — has posted 93.on SWE-bench Verified and 94.on GPQA Diamond in gated evaluations. When it reaches public availability, it will reset performance expectations again. Grok 5 from xAI is expected in Q2. DeepSeek V4 full release is imminent. GPT-5.5 will likely be followed by further iterations before summer. [...] AI.cc is a unified AI API aggregation platform headquartered in Singapore, providing developers and enterprises with seamless access to 300+ AI models — including GPT-5.5 (OpenAI), Claude Opus 4.7 (Anthropic), Gemini 3.1 Pro (Google), DeepSeek V4 (DeepSeek), Llama 4 (Meta), Qwen 3.6-Plus (Alibaba), Gemma 4 (Google), GLM-5.1 (Zhipu AI), and more — through a single OpenAI-compatible API. The platform supports text, image, video, voice, code, embedding, and OCR model categories. AI.cc also offers the OpenClaw AI agent framework, enterprise plans with SLA guarantees, AI application development services, AI Translator API, web scraping services, and GEO-optimized SEO and PR services.

title: GPT-5.5 Released by OpenAI Targets Coding and Research
summary: GPT-5.5 Released by OpenAI Targets Coding and Research GPT-5.5 Has Arrived: OpenAI’s Latest AI Model Targets Coding and Research Try Our Free Tools! Master the web with Free Tools that work as hard as you do.
content: GPT-5.5 Released by OpenAI Targets Coding and Research [...] GPT-5.5 Has Arrived: OpenAI’s Latest AI Model Targets Coding and Research [...] OpenAI Unveils GPT-5.5: A Revolutionary AI Model for Enhanced Utility [...] New Delhi: OpenAI has officially introduced GPT-5.5, its latest AI architecture for ChatGPT and Codex users, merely two months following the release of GPT-5.4. [...] Designed to accommodate intricate tasks, the model excels in coding, online research, data analysis, document generation, and various computer-related functions. [...] The rollout commenced on April 24 (IST) for paying subscribers of ChatGPT and Codex, with an API interface anticipated to launch promptly. [...] This update transcends the conventional model adjustments familiar to users. GPT-5.5 is presented as a more autonomous aide, equipped to tackle convoluted assignments, utilize tools efficiently, verify its outputs, and navigate through processes with minimal supervision. [...] Introducing GPT-5.5A new class of intelligence for real work and powering agents, built to understand complex goals, utilize tools, verify its findings, and execute multiple tasks seamlessly. [...] Key Enhancements in GPT-5.5 [...] According to OpenAI, GPT-5.5 excels in “agentic” tasks, allowing it to manage extensive projects and navigate through complex processes more adeptly than earlier models. [...] The firm highlights [...] the same real-world [...] as GPT-5. [...] , despite its heightened intelligence. Furthermore, it consumes fewer tokens for [...] Codex tasks, an important metric for prolonged [...] GPT-5.5: Advancements in Coding and Office Functions [...] The most significant improvements appear in the coding realm. OpenAI describes GPT-5.5 as its most potent agentic coding model to date. Early users report that it excels in maintaining context across extensive systems, accurately identifying problem origins, and anticipating subsequent issues that may arise post-fix. [...] Current Access to GPT-5.5 [...] GPT-5.5 is being gradually made available to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users of ChatGPT and Codex. In ChatGPT, GPT-5.5 Pro will be accessible for Pro, Business, and Enterprise users. [...] Meanwhile, in Codex, GPT-5.5 is being rolled out with a 400K context window across Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, Edu, and Go plans. [...] For developers, OpenAI has indicated that GPT-5.5 API access will be forthcoming at a rate of $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens. [...] Meanwhile, GPT-5.5 Pro API pricing is projected at $30 per million input tokens and $180 per million output tokens.
